steps: Lather for 15 seconds, covering all surfaces
Turn off faucet with paper towel
Rub over all hand and wrist surfaces
Continue for at least 15 seconds until dry
Open inner package with cuff end closest
Pick up glove at folded cuff edge
Insert gloved hand beneath folded edge of second glove

principles: Touch sterile item with another sterile item only
Any partially unwrapped sterile package is contaminated
If in doubt about sterility, it is unsterile
Outer 1-inch margin of sterile area is contamination zone
Any opened sterile item left unattended is contaminated
Coughing/sneezing over sterile field causes contamination
Sterile items below waist level are contaminated

steps: Remove soiled linen (roll soiled side in)
Place clean bottom sheet with center longitudinal fold
Tuck at foot with mitered corners
Place pillow with open end away from door
Raise bed, cover patient with bath blanket
Lower side rail, roll patient toward opposite side
Roll soiled bottom sheet close to patient
Apply clean bottom sheet on vacant side
Raise side rail, move to opposite side
Roll patient over mound of sheets
Pull clean sheet from under patient

patient_positions.description: Semi-prone, lying on left side with right knee drawn up

principles: Use longest and strongest muscles of arms and legs
Keep feet apart for broad base of support
Bend knees and keep back straight when lifting
Push, pull, or roll rather than lift when possible
Avoid twisting and stretching during work

steps: Raise bed, cover with bath blanket
Turn patient to side, wash back
Instill water and suction with bulb syringe
Never use water if patient cannot swallow
Store in cold water in labeled cup

SOAPIER_charting.O: Objective data (vital signs, physical exam findings)

example_entries.entry: S: 'I don't feel well', O: Temperature 39.3°C orally, A: Fever, P: Offer extra fluids and monitor, I: 750 mL fluids in 8 hours, temp q4h, E: Temperature reduced to 37.4°C, R: Increase fluid to 1000 mL per shift

S: 'Suture is very painful' (pain scale 6/10), O: Facial grimace, irritability, A: Acute pain post C-section, P: Patient will report pain relief to 4/10 within 8 hours, I: Quiet environment, deep breathing, support area, E: Goal met, pain decreased to 4/10
